content:
Perfect! The server starts up very quickly (within 1 second). I can see:
1. "Test mode enabled" is logged
2. "Server started" appears almost immediately
3. Health check returns `{"status":"ok","uptime_seconds":3}`

The 403 errors are from the real MTA feeds - these happen in the background after the server starts. Let me check the health endpoint implementation and verify it's working correctly.

content:
Good, the server is starting successfully (tests are running, not timing out). The test failures are unrelated to server startup - they're about missing page elements. Let me focus on the server configuration issues.

Let me check the current configuration against the comments and verify the timeout values:

args.activeForm: Verifying and stabilizing playwright webServer configuration
args.description: Verify the playwright webServer timeout, health check, and TEST_MODE propagation to ensure reliable server startup for e2e tests.
args.subject: Verify and stabilize playwright webServer configuration

output: Task #1 created successfully: Verify and stabilize playwright webServer configuration

output: (Bash completed with no output)
Wasted call — file unchanged since your last Read. Refer to that earlier tool_result instead.

args.command:
cd /home/coding/mta-my-way
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  echo "=== Startup test $i ==="
  # Start server in background, capture PID
  TEST_MODE=true npx tsx packages/server/src/index.ts > /tmp/server-$i.log 2>&1 &
  SERVER_PID=$!
  
  # Wait for health endpoint to respond
  start=$(date +%s)
  waited=0
  while [ $waited -lt 10 ]; do
    if curl -s http://localhost:3001/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
      end=$(date +%s)
      echo "✓ Server responded in $((end - start))s"
      break
    fi
    sleep 0.2
    waited=$((waited + 1))
  done
  
  if [ $waited -ge 10 ]; then
    echo "✗ Server did not respond within 10s"
  fi
  
  # Stop server
  kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null
  wait $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null
  
  sleep 1
done

content: Perfect! The server starts consistently and immediately (0-1 seconds). Now let me verify TEST_MODE is actually bypassing the rate limiter:

args.command:
TEST_MODE=true npx tsx packages/server/src/index.ts > /tmp/test-mode-test.log 2>&1 &
SERVER_PID=$!
sleep 3

# Make multiple rapid requests to a rate-limited endpoint
echo "Testing rate limiter bypass with TEST_MODE=true..."
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  response=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3001/api/stations -o /tmp/stations-$i.json)
  echo "Request $i: HTTP $response"
done

# Check logs for TEST_MODE confirmation
grep -i "test mode" /tmp/test-mode-test.log

kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null
wait $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null

content: Excellent! TEST_MODE is confirmed working - all 5 requests succeeded with HTTP 200 and the logs show "Test mode enabled". Now let me verify port conflict detection:

args.command:
# Start a server on port 3001
npx tsx packages/server/src/index.ts > /tmp/blocking-server.log 2>&1 &
BLOCKING_PID=$!
sleep 2

# Try to run the port checker
echo "Testing port conflict detection..."
cd /home/coding/mta-my-way/tests/e2e && npx tsx helpers/check-port.ts
PORT_CHECK_EXIT=$?

# Clean up
kill $BLOCKING_PID 2>/dev/null
wait $BLOCKING_PID 2>/dev/null

echo "Port check exit code: $PORT_CHECK_EXIT"
if [ $PORT_CHECK_EXIT -eq 1 ]; then
  echo "✓ Port conflict detected correctly"
else
  echo "✗ Port conflict NOT detected"
fi
